2026-02-14 16:55:21.169300+00:00;13385;model;The following algorithm implements a **Vectorized Self-Adaptive Differential Evolution (JADE-like variant)**.
1.  **Mutation Strategy (`DE/current-to-pbest/1`)**: Unlike the standard `DE/rand/1` (random exploration) used in previous iterations
column2: this strategy directs the search by mutating individuals towards the best solutions currently found (top $p\%$). This significantly accelerates convergence while maintaining robustness.

column1: 2.  **Self-Adaptation**: It employs the jDE mechanism where mutation factor $F$ and crossover rate $CR$ are encoded into individuals and evolve. This removes the need for manual hyperparameter tuning.
3.  **Vectorization**: The generation of mutant vectors and crossover operations are fully vectorized using NumPy
column2: maximizing the number of generations possible within the time limit.

column1: 4.  **Restart Mechanism**: Detects population stagnation (when fitness variance drops) and restarts the population while preserving the elite solution
column2: ensuring the algorithm doesn't get stuck in local optima.

# ""p-best"" rate: Controls greediness. Top 10% guide the mutation.
# Restart threshold: If fitness spread < this
# Each individual has its own F and CR.
best_sol = None # Keep track of the elite solution
# If time runs out during initialization
return best_fitness if best_sol is not None else float('inf')
# If the population has converged (variance is near zero)

# Randomly reset F and CR for a subset of the population to maintain diversity in search logic

# Select X_pbest: Randomly chosen from top p% for each individual
# Note: In pure theory r1 != r2 != i

column2: ensuring the algorithm can effectively explore new regions if it gets stuck.
